name: Dolby Laboratories, Inc.
business: Dolby Laboratories licenses audio and video technologies to device manufacturers and sells cinema hardware and services to movie exhibitors. The majority of revenue (93% in fiscal 2025) comes from licensing branded technologies such as Dolby Atmos and Dolby Vision, as well as patents for standardized codecs like AAC and HEVC.
sellsTo: Other (Dolby Cinema, gaming consoles, automotive, patent pool admin fees)

exposures.sentence: Gaming consoles such as the Sony PlayStation and the Microsoft Xbox use DD+ to support gaming content and streaming for movie and television content.
We generate revenue from the automotive industry primarily through the adoption of Dolby Atmos in cars.
We have an established global presence and broad adoption of our branded audio and patent licensing technologies in broadcast services and devices, which primarily include TVs and STBs.

Our future success depends on our ability to enhance our technologies and products and to develop new technologies and products that address market needs in a timely manner, including the development of technologies and products that incorporate rapidly developing generative artificial intelligence and other artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies ("AI/ML").

catalysts.sentence: In fiscal 2025, we announced Dolby Vision 2, which is expected to improve picture quality and unleash the full capabilities of modern TVs, by automatically adjusting contrast via ambient light detection, optimizing motion control for sports and gaming content, and tone mapping that enables creators to take full advantage of the latest advancements in higher end TV displays.

catalysts.event: Hisense and TCL to release TVs supporting Dolby Vision 2
catalysts.sentence: Hisense and TCL announced that they will release TVs that support Dolby Vision 2.

In September 2025, we initiated restructuring actions in order to centralize teams into fewer locations to provide better access to talent pools, encourage multi-disciplinary collaboration, and simplify operations.

catalysts.event: Restructuring initiated to align R&D resources
catalysts.sentence: In November 2024, we initiated restructuring actions with the purpose of aligning our R&D resources, and to a lesser extent our S&M resources, with our highest strategic priorities.

catalysts.event: Microsoft and Google join Via LA HEVC/VVC patent pool as licensees
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, Microsoft and Google joined Via LA’s HEVC/VVC patent pool as licensees.

catalysts.event: Expected revenue from Video Distribution Program in fiscal 2026
catalysts.sentence: We expect to start generating revenue from the program in fiscal 2026 and, as streaming continues to grow in popularity, we expect this new patent pool will be an important growth driver for Dolby.

catalysts.event: AMC agreement to add 40 Dolby Cinemas through end of calendar 2027
catalysts.sentence: In fiscal 2025, we announced with AMC that we will add an additional 40 Dolby Cinemas at AMC locations in the U.S. through the end of calendar year 2027.

catalysts.event: Launch of Dolby Cinema in India with six exhibitors by end of fiscal 2026
catalysts.sentence: We also announced that we are launching Dolby Cinema in India this year, beginning with six exhibitors that are expected to be open by the end of fiscal 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Over one billion mobile devices, approximately 200 million TVs, and approximately 90 million cars sold annually in 2024.
tamClaims.sentence: For example, in 2024, there were over one billion mobile devices, approximately 200 million TVs and, according to Wards Intelligence, an Auto Research firm, approximately 90 million cars sold.
